redb_use: CodeDB source_blobs — content-addressed captured file bytes via codedb_store_redb (redb-backed BlobStore)
current_status: implemented + parity-tested (blobstore_parity.rs); durable DEFAULT store today
owner: codedb capture CLI (single writer per store file)
durability_requirement: DURABLE today — this is the operating source-of-truth for captured blobs unless STORE-001 reassigns it
identity: SHA-256 content hash of file bytes (byte-exact)
sync_edge: one-way capture -> store; peer backend codedb_store_pg::PgStore implements the SAME BlobStore contract (selectable, not a tier above)
crash_replay_rule: redb single-file ACID; re-open recovers committed state; re-capture from working tree fills gaps
recovery_source: redb file + working tree; parity gate codedb_store_pg/tests/blobstore_parity.rs
observability_signal: capture receipts, resume skip-set, store schema version (StoreMetadataRow)
relationship_to_postgres_and_codedb: PEER of PgStore under one BlobStore trait; redb is NOT proven transient — silently reclassifying it to buffer-only would erase a durable use (forbidden by PGAUTH-002 gate)

redb_use: envctl migration_db — replayable migration/orchestration state (targets, runs, run_events, approvals, checkpoints, rollbacks) in redb
current_status: implemented; durable runtime store for envctl (adopted via ADOPT-002)
lifetime: durable across the migration lifecycle (long-lived audit history)
durability_requirement: DURABLE today — separate from CodeDB; envctl uses redb independently for migration/state
sync_edge: if PG becomes canonical macro-history: one-way promote run history to PG audit tables; envctl redb stays runtime store or is cut over per-class
crash_replay_rule: redb single-file ACID; envctl's replayable migration engine re-derives state
relationship_to_postgres_and_codedb: SEPARATE durable redb owner from CodeDB; must be decided, not erased (REDB-CLAIM-020 qualified names this exact use)

redb_use: High-frequency agent scratchpad — transient per-agent thoughts/state changes logged to redb without network lag
current_status: NOT a current adopted LifeOS role; feasible mechanism only
owner: owning agent runtime (single writer per scratchpad)
durability_requirement: TRANSIENT proposal — no adopted retention/privacy/promotion/recovery decision makes this its exclusive role
sync_edge: one-way: scratchpad -> optional curated flush to macro-state; never authoritative
crash_replay_rule: safe to discard on crash (transient by definition); recompute from agent context
recovery_source: none required (disposable); re-created by the agent
observability_signal: scratchpad write latency (RUVPG-CLAIM-018 microsecond claim is UNVERIFIED)
relationship_to_postgres_and_codedb: local to agent; unrelated to CodeDB blobs; may flush summaries to PostgreSQL macro-state one-way

redb_use: Embedding cache — model-output cache keyed by BLAKE3 content hash to avoid duplicate inference
current_status: feasible mechanism; no current model-aware cache schema proven
durability_requirement: DISPOSABLE cache — a miss is always safe (recompute); never authority
identity: BLAKE3 hash of (content + model + version + config) — collision/invalidation handling unproven
sync_edge: one-way: canonical input -> cache entry; read-only on hit
crash_replay_rule: safe to discard on crash; recompute on miss
relationship_to_postgres_and_codedb: derived from canonical source rows (PGAUTH-001 semantic-vectors row); never a competing authority

redb_use: Application-level WAL / outbox — redb buffers outgoing PostgreSQL operations during PG latency spikes or outages, retaining until connectivity returns
current_status: feasible; NOT PostgreSQL WAL; no ordered/idempotent redb->PG outage protocol implemented
durability_requirement: DURABLE-UNTIL-ACKED buffer — must not be conflated with PG WAL or with authority; capacity/backpressure/ordering/idempotency undefined today
identity: monotonic sequence key + operation idempotency key (undefined today)
sync_edge: one-way: local op -> outbox -> replay to PostgreSQL with ack; retry + dedup on reconnect
crash_replay_rule: on crash: replay unacked entries in order; idempotency prevents double-apply (protocol NOT yet built)
recovery_source: redb file replay -> PostgreSQL; PostgreSQL is the destination authority
observability_signal: outbox depth, replay lag, poison-record count
relationship_to_postgres_and_codedb: SUBORDINATE to PostgreSQL: buffers writes destined for PG; PG remains canonical; envctl is NOT the synchronizing writer (AUTH-CLAIM-001 refuted)

redb_use: Intermediate ingestion buffer — redb as the buffer between parser output and durable PostgreSQL state (NotebookLM 'migration bridge' framing)
current_status: CONFLICT: the source's buffer-only framing contradicts PGREDB-001, where redb is the current DURABLE default, not a transient bridge; nu_plugin does NOT buffer parsed blocks into redb
lifetime: transient (buffer) in the proposal vs durable in current reality
durability_requirement: CONTESTED — proposal says transient buffer; current CodeDB uses redb durably; STORE-001 must resolve, not assume
sync_edge: proposed: parser -> redb buffer -> PostgreSQL; current reality: capture -> durable redb (or PgStore) with no plugin->redb buffering
crash_replay_rule: if buffer: replay to PG; if durable (today): recover from redb file
relationship_to_postgres_and_codedb: DIRECT CONTRADICTION between claim map (transient buffer) and verified code (durable default + plugin never buffers to redb) — the central STORE-001 question

redb_use: Shared redb state — LifeOS UI reads the same redb file agents write, for low-latency swarm-status views over UDS or shared redb state
current_status: owner-decision-required; not implemented; competes with a UDS-only control path
owner: agents write; LifeOS UI reads (multi-reader, potential shared-file coupling)
durability_requirement: READ-VIEW proposal — must not create a second writable authority over agent state; overlap risk flagged by the source itself
sync_edge: read-only projection for UI; the interactive control path may instead be UDS with redb-backed views (PGAUTH-004 territory)
crash_replay_rule: UI view is disposable; underlying agent state recovers per its own family
recovery_source: re-read from the canonical agent state; view holds no authority
relationship_to_postgres_and_codedb: READER of agent/redb state for UI only; must not become a dual-writer; relationship to CodeDB is none (different data class)
